revision.text:
[[ja:仏法紹隆寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:仏法紹隆寺山門.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Suwa|qid=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.0210778|N|138.1384306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Buppou Shoryuji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:仏法紹隆寺山門.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_desc = Buppou Shoryuji Sanmon (Mountain Gate) | location = {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa City|1=Suwa, Nagano}} Shiga 4373 | coordinates = | Mountain_name = {{ill|Washizawa Sōgon-san|ja|鷲澤荘厳山|lt=Washizawa Sōgon-san|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washizawa Sōgon-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Sect_name = {{ill|Daikokuzō-in|ja|大虚空蔵院|lt=Daikokuzō-in|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikokuzō-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Latitude_deg = 36 | Latitude_min = 1 | Latitude_sec = 15.88 | Longitude_deg = 138 | Longitude_min = 8 | Longitude_sec = 18.35 | Sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} | deity = {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}
{{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} | consecration_year_year = Daidō 1 (806) | Founder = Tradition: {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|lt=Sakaue Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} | Official_name = Washizawa Sōgon-san Daikokuzō-in Buppou Shoryuji | Alias = Buppou-ji, {{ill|Jigan-ji|ja|慈眼寺|lt=Jigan-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigan-ji|1=Jigenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495027}} | Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Ina Suwa 88 Pilgrimage|ja|伊那諏訪八十八霊場|lt=Ina Suwa 88 Pilgrimage|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Suwa 88 Pilgrimage|1=Ina Suwa 88 Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128866411}} No.23 | Cultural_properties= }} '''Buppou Shoryuji''' (仏法紹隆寺, Buppou Shoryuji) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|qid=Q5393308|lt=Zen Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Buddhism|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa City|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11058522}} is {{ill|Washizawa Sōgon-san|ja|鷲澤荘厳山|lt=Washizawa Sōgon-san|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washizawa Sōgon-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and its {{ill|Sect name|ja|院号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sect name|1=Sect name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658114}} is {{ill|Daikokuzō-in|ja|大虚空蔵院|lt=Daikokuzō-in|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikokuzō-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is commonly known as Buppou-ji or {{ill|Jigan-ji|ja|慈眼寺|lt=Jigan-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigan-ji|1=Jigenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495027}}. [[File:仏法紹隆寺大イチョウ.jpg|thumb|180x180px|Buppou Shoryuji's large ginkgo tree. There is a tradition that considers the male ginkgo tree at Buppou Shoryuji and the female ginkgo tree on the premises of the Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine in Suwa City's Nakasu district as "husband and wife".]] == History == According to temple tradition, in Daidō 1 (806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|lt=Sakaue Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}, serving as a court lady to the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, established Buppou Shoryuji along with {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Shinjūji|1=Jingū-ji}} (which was abolished during the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}). It was named {{ill|Jigan-ji|ja|慈眼寺|lt=Jigan-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigan-ji|1=Jigenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495027}} as a branch temple (別当寺) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. During the {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}}, under the guidance of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi), {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Shinjūji|1=Jingū-ji}} became a temple spreading the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} and {{ill|Jigan-ji|ja|慈眼寺|lt=Jigan-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigan-ji|1=Jigenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495027}} served as a school for Shingon Buddhism, designated as a {{ill|Jōhō Tanrinsho|ja|常法談林所|lt=Jōhō Tanrinsho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōhō Tanrinsho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 檀林|1=danrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544577}}, and hailed as "the foremost of the four temple schools in Shinshu". In Bunwa 1 (1352), under Master {{ill|Shun-Kai Hōshi|ja|俊海法師|lt=Shun-Kai Hōshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shun-Kai Hōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the temple site was relocated, and in Eiroku 3 (1560), during Master {{ill|Sonzō Hōshi|ja|尊朝法師|lt=Sonzō Hōshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonzō Hōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s tenure, it moved to its current location and was renamed "Buppou Shoryuji". During the Edo period, it became a prayer temple for the {{ill|Suwa Takashima Domain|ja|諏訪高島藩|lt=Suwa Takashima Domain|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Takashima Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the Taishō period, it encountered a great fire, destroying the main hall, kitchen, founding hall, Kongōden, and corridors, but the main hall, kitchen, and founding hall were rebuilt. In Heisei 1 (1989), {{ill|Chieidō|ja|智映堂|lt=Chieidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chieidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established. [[File:諏訪大明神御本地普賢菩薩像 - Suwa Daimyōjin as Bodhisattva Samantabhadra (Fugen Bosatsu).jpg|thumb|Chieidō of Buppou Shoryuji. It is believed that the large ginkgo tree on the grounds of the Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine in Suwa City's Nakasu district and the male ginkgo tree at Buppou Shoryuji are "husband and wife" trees.]] == Overview == The main deities enshrined are the esoteric Buddha {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. According to Genso no Kōsei-in's "Shinshu Shimuki" of {{ill|Daigo-ji Risei-in|ja|醍醐寺理性院|lt=Daigo-ji Risei-in|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji Risei-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kyoto, in Tenbun 2 (1533), during the transmission and consecration (灌頂) of doctrines and bonds at {{ill|Bun'ei-ji|ja|文永寺|lt=Bun'ei-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'ei-ji|1=Bun'ei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30319276}} in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|qid=Q841129|lt=Iida City|1=Iida, Nagano}}, it is recorded that "the ritual implements were brought from Suwa's Buppou Shoryuji." This indicates that Buppou Shoryuji is a venerable ancient temple that houses significant ritual implements, documents, and sutras from the Heian to Sengoku periods. Climbing up from the old {{ill|Kōshū Kaidō|en|Kōshū Kaidō|qid=Q1156682|lt=Kōshū Kaidō|1=Kōshū Kaidō}}, there is a mountain gate that used to be the main gate of {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Shinjūji|1=Jingū-ji}}, and within the temple grounds is a large ginkgo tree (a cultural property of Suwa City, estimated age over 500 years). In front of the main hall, there is {{ill|Seitendo|ja|聖天堂|lt=Seitendo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seitendo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Fudōdō|ja|不動堂|lt=Fudōdō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudōdō|1=Fudōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361597}} on the left side, and the kitchen on the right side. Behind the main hall's {{ill|Ikezen Shūyō-shiki Teien|ja|池泉回遊式庭園|lt=Ikezen Shūyō-shiki Teien|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikezen Shūyō-shiki Teien|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 回遊式庭園|1=Strolling garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420231}} is the {{ill|Fugen-dō|ja|普賢堂|lt=Fugen-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1975 from {{ill|Koyasan Tokyo Betsuin|en|Koyasan Tokyo Betsuin|qid=Q6435146|lt=Koyasan Tokyo Betsuin|1=Koyasan Tokyo Betsuin}}. This building is a {{ill|Tented roof|en|Tented roof|qid=Q149276|lt=Hōkei-zukuri|1=Tented roof}} structure built in Enpō 1 (1673), featuring a pseudo-jewel enshrouded corridor with a one-room worship hall attached. Inside the hall, the main deity of Shinjūji's {{ill|Fugen-dō|ja|普賢堂|lt=Fugen-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|qid=Q868306|lt=Fugen Bosatsu|1=Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)}} (a cultural property of Suwa City), is enshrined as the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honchi-butsu|1=Honji suijaku}} donated by {{ill|Chikuatsu Atsuyuki|ja|知久敦幸|lt=Chikuatsu Atsuyuki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuatsu Atsuyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in En'ei 1 (1293) as the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honchi-butsu|1=Honji suijaku}} of {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪明神|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2370082}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|ja|普賢菩薩騎象像|lt=Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県指定文化財一覧|lt=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|1=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654318}}) from Shinjūji's {{ill|Nyo-hōin|ja|如法院|lt=Nyo-hōin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyo-hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|ja|普賢菩薩騎象像|lt=Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen Bosatsu Riding Elephant Statue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has also been relocated here.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://archive.md/pByUp |title=...|qq=}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 15 year (1882), the Seiryōdō built that year enshrined a separate deity of the Rinzai School from {{ill|Daigo-ji Risei-in|ja|醍醐寺理性院|lt=Daigo-ji Risei-in|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji Risei-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the temple houses cultural properties including 600 scrolls of the ancient {{ill|Sutra of the Great Prajñāpāramitā|ja|大般若経|lt=Sutra of the Great Prajñāpāramitā|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of the Great Prajñāpāramitā|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大般若波羅蜜多経|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15905785}}, 13 ancient sutras from the Eiroku period onward, an image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} from the Kamakura period (a {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県指定文化財一覧|lt=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|1=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654318}}), and paintings of the {{ill|Juroku Zenjin|ja|十六善神|lt=Juroku Zenjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juroku Zenjin|1=Sixteen dharmapālas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18555735}}. == Access == * From {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|Kami-Suwa Station|en|Kami-Suwa Station|qid=Q844049|lt=Kami-Suwa Station|1=Kami-Suwa Station}} via {{ill|Suwa Bus|en|Suwa Bus|qid=Q7650538|lt=Suwa Bus|1=Suwa Bus}} "Shiga Branch Office Front" stop, approximately 10 minutes on foot. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shinshu no Bunka Series: Tera to Jinja』|year=1981|publisher=Shinshu Mainichi Shimbunsha|qq=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Tanbou Shinshu no Koji: Tendai-shū/Shingon-shū』|year=1996|publisher=Kyodo Publishing|qq=}} == See Also == * [[Suwa City#Temples|Temples of Suwa City]] {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture|qq=}} {{Buddhism-stub|qq=}} {{Suwa Faith|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Suwa Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|仏法紹隆寺|version=102309770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:仏法紹隆寺]] {{wikidata link|Q111436290}} [[Category:Tamura storytelling]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nagano Prefecture]]
